title: Fusion
description: Fusion is a you-agent-factory technique that runs two model passes on the same request: a first pass drafts an answer and a second pass refines it.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Fusion is a you-agent-factory technique that runs two model passes on the same request: a first pass drafts an answer and a second pass refines it. Customers meet this shape as packaged @you/fusion.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: A second pass can catch gaps the first draft missed, and callers can use different providers or models for draft versus refine.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: The caller supplies input; the first pass drafts an answer; the second pass refines that draft into the customer-facing result.

comparedToNearbyTechniques.title: Compared To Nearby Techniques
comparedToNearbyTechniques.body: Fusion is a draft-then-refine two-pass flow, not a role-split write-review loop, not a persistent keep-going loop, and not a plan-then-execute split.
